ogTitle: West Kempsey vs West Pennant Hills: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in West Kempsey and West Pennant Hills.
heading: West Kempsey vs West Pennant Hills — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between West Kempsey, New South Wales and West Pennant Hills, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: West Kempsey has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to West Pennant Hills (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is West Kempsey or West Pennant Hills safer from flooding?
faq.answer: West Kempsey has a flood risk score of 29/100, while West Pennant Hills scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: West Kempsey scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. West Pennant Hills scores 60/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in West Kempsey or West Pennant Hills?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. West Kempsey has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.12% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
